text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little town on the outskirts of a bustling city, there was a big oak tree nestled in a small clearing. Every year, on St. Bartholomeus' Day, the townsfolk would gather around the tree to celebrate.

On this particular year, a young girl named Lucy was inspired by the tree to write a letter. She mailed it many times throughout the day, her pen moving eagerly across paper. When night fell, lightning streaked across the sky and Lucy dreamt that the magical oak spoke to her through glowing eyes.

The next morning, Lucy awoke to find an envelope at her doorstep. Opening it, she found an envelope sealed with a paw print. Inside was a bird feather. With a mix of wonder and curiosity, she plucked the feather to decorate her bookshelf.

One day during a family road trip, Lucy pointed at the feather in her bookshelf and declared with glee, "Look mommy! A bird feather!"
